forklift-operator-refresher-training.title: Forklift Operator Refresher Training
forklift-operator-refresher-training.efgCourseDuration: • 12 Hours (11 training hours + 1 assessment hour (theory and practical))

sections.overview:
• The Forklift Operator Refresher Course aims to provide trainees with the skills and knowledge required to operate a forklift safely.
  • The validity period for a forklift operator is 3 years. Thereafter, forklift operators shall attend the Forklift Operator Refresher Training course once every 3 yearly (SS573:2012 for forklift retraining is every 3 years)
sections.objectives:
1. Online Training (Asynchronous e-Learning for 7 modules)
  1. Relevant Workplace Safety & Health (WSH) Legislations/Singapore Standards SS573, applicable to forklift operation
  2. Refresh on the correct interpretation of the Load Chart
  3. Forklift Stability
  4. Pre-Operational & Post Checks on a Forklift Machine
  5. Techniques and requirements for safe operations
  6. Highlights on the common safety lapses by forklift operators and the consequences.
  7. Safety Rules (Do’s and Don’ts)
  • Workshop (In person training)
  1. 4 case studies
  2. Forklift Practical inclusive of 15 mins practical assessment
sections.attendance: 100% for asynchronous learning and practical training cum assessment
sections.trainerRatio:
• Workshop (In person training) 1:20
  • Practical 1:1 (Group of 5 trainees per forklift machine per trainer)
sections.assessment:
• The trainee must attain 60% pass for the online formative assessment for Forklift Operator Refresher Training and 100% pass for the practical assessments to be issued the “Certificate of Successful Completion”.
  • The number of attempts a candidate is allowed to be assessed in the written assessment is ONE. The candidate certified “NOT YET COMPETENT” after the assessment must be re-coursed.

Practical assessment: 15 mins/Trainee, as stated below:

  • Inspect forklift machine (2 mins)
  • Carry out forklift operations (11 mins)
  • Parking (in & out of parking lot) (2mins)
  • Manoeuvring (S and L shape) with and without load (3 mins)
  • Loading and Unloading (6 mins)
  • Reinstate and handover forklift (2 mins)
sections.certifications:
• Upon completing the E-learning 07 modules and passing the formative assessment, participants will receive a Certificate of Completion from EFG Training Services Pte Ltd and be eligible to attend the in-person Workshop, Practical training and assessment.
  • Upon meeting at least 100% attendance of In-person workshop, Practical training and assessment, participants will receive a Certificate of Successful Completion from EFG Training Services Pte Ltd valid for 3 years.

sections.overview: This course has been designed to meet the minimum standard for Confined Space Rescue, as recommended in the WSH (Confined Space) Regulations 2009, SS 568 : 2011, Technical Advisory on Working Safely in Confined Spaces and Confined Space Safety Guidebook for Supervisors and Workers.
sections.audience: The course is for anyone involved in the supervision, planning / assessment or management of confined space work activities.
sections.objectives:
The aim of the course is to provide Learners with the requisite skills, knowledge to manage and  rescue, so that work can be carried out safely. At the end of the course, the Learner shall be able to:

  • Identify, explain and apply the relevant Workplace Safety and Health (WSH)   Legislations / Approved Code of Practice / Singapore Standards applicable to Confined Space
  • Identify and explain the Roles & Responsibilities of stakeholders, competent and responsible persons.
  • Identify hazards, evaluate the risk, and implement control measures for Confined Space Work activities
  • Review the necessary fall preventive / protective control measures for safe execution of Confined Space Work activities
  • Establish, evaluate, review and implement a Confined Space Rescue Plan
  • Establish and implement a system for an effective rescue and control of Confined Space Work risks
  • Demonstrate competency in donning, checking and correcting of Confined Space Rescue Equipment and Responding to a confined space emergency and Perform Confined Space Rescue.
sections.prerequisites: The Learner should have at least WSQ ESS Workplace Literacy and Numeracy Level 4 and above or equivalent and the ability to carry out hazards identification, risk assessment, and risk control measures.
sections.trainerRatio:
1: 20 Theory & 1: 20 Theory Assessment

1: 10 Practical & 1: 1 Practical Assessment
sections.assessment:
Comprising both Theory and Practical Session

Written Test (WT) / Practical Performance (PP)

Theory Examination (WT): 70%

Practical Assessments (PP): 100%
sections.methodology: Slides presentation, sharing of best practice and incident. Hands on practical session.
sections.certifications: Certificates and Cards shall be endorsed by EFG Training Services Private Ltd and issued to participants at the end of course.

sections.audience:
Confined Space Attendant, Confined Space Entrant (Worker), Technician, Supervisor

Entry Requirement

Learners are assumed to:

  • Be able to listen, read, speak and write English
  • Be able to manipulate numbers
  • Be able to follow written and oral work instructions
  • Must complete Perform Work In Confined Space Operation course
sections.overview: Upon completion of this training, participants will be able to demonstrate Knowledge and skills required to safely manage entry into confined spaces. Specifically, he/she will be able to identify hazards associated with confined spaces, monitor the number of entrants moving in and out of the space, ensure adequate ventilation and atmospheric testing, use personal protective equipment (PPE), communicate effectively with the entrants and other team members, activate the emergency response plan when needed, and perform non-entry rescue operations. He/she will also be able to demonstrate an understanding of relevant regulations and standards governing confined space operations and recognize the importance of continuous monitoring and regular training to maintain a safe work environment.
sections.objectives:
• Introduction
  • Identify duties and responsibilities of all personnel involved in confined space work under the WSH Legislations and Codes of Practice
  • Overview of confined space
  • Common confined space hazards and their characteristics
  • Gas Detection Instruments for atmospheric monitoring
  • Comply with safe work procedures when working in confined space
  • Continuous monitoring of the confined space and entrants
  • Assist in implementing a communication and emergency response plan
  • Case Studies
sections.methodology:
Blended learning – Classroom – Face to face Training and hands on Practical Training with Gas meter

Learning Environment: ·

  • Room with the capacity for up to 20 candidates for conduct of written assessment
  • Room or place to be used must be away from noise or interruptions

1:20 for Theory Lesson & Assessment

  • Practical assessment – Group Role Play &
  • Oral Questions – competency based

sections.audience: The course is suitable for Competent chemical handlers, Process plant Supervisors, Process plant Technicians, Process plant Workers, WSH Committee Members, Laboratory Personnel and WSH professionals.
sections.prerequisites:
Competent Chemical handlers, Process Plant Supervisors, Process plant Technicians, Process plant Workers, WSH committee members, Laboratory personnel and WSH professionals.

Course Overview

The Chemical Awareness Safety Training program aims to educate industrial workers on the risks associated with chemicals and promote safe handling practices. Participants will learn key aspects such as hazard identification, interpreting Safety Data Sheets (SDS), conducting risk assessments, and best practices for safe storage and handling of chemicals. The course emphasizes the use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE), emergency response protocols, and includes practical case studies to enhance learning. Assessment ensures participants can apply these safety measures effectively in their workplaces.
sections.objectives:
• New Workplace Health and Safety Act (WSHA) Framework and legal requirements
  • Overview of chemical hazards and their associated risks
  • Risk assessment process
  • Safe work practices for chemical handling, transporting, storage and disposal
  • Chemical emergency response procedures
  • Case Studies
sections.methodology: • Blended learning – Class Room Facto face Training
sections.assessment: Written Assessment – 0.5 Hour – 20 MCQs – 60% competency based

sections.objectives: The main objective of the Working-At-Height Rescue training is to train the participants to gain the knowledge and the skills needed to successfully rescue the people involved in the event of a fall emergency during any Working-At-height related activity.
sections.courseContent:
• Learn the use of height rescue equipment
  • Learn the tying of various knots for height rescue
  • Learn to perform rope rescue activities to recover fallen or incapacitated worker(s)
  • Learn the safe use and inspection of equipment used for vertical rope rescue activities.
  • Learn the rope rescue techniques, anchor selection, rigging mechanical advantage and stretcher operations involved with rope rescue activities.
  • Acquire the knowledge and skills required for risk assessment and casualty management involved with all rope rescue activities.
sections.prerequisites:
Participants should have attended any working at height related courses.

Participants should preferably have 2 years of working at height experience.

confined-space-management.title: Confined Space Management (Assess Confined Space for Safe Entry and Work)

sections.overview: This unit covers basic principles in working within/around confined space. It includes the knowledge and skills for the use of appropriate gas testing equipment and methods to obtain representative results for the assessment of atmospheric condition in confined space for safe entry and work. The skill and knowledge covered will enable the learners to be appointed as confined space safety assessor under the relevant regulations.
sections.audience:
This course is mandatory for those assessor, who is assessing confined space for entry and doing the works in the confined in Singapore.

The target audience of this competency unit may include:

Persons who want to obtain the knowledge to be competent for taking up appointment of a confined space safety assessor Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) professionals such as WSH Coordinators, Officers, and Auditors.
sections.objectives:
1. Prepare for confined space assessment with persons involved in confined space safety management in accordance with the applicable legal and other requirements.

2. Identify hazards and take measures to prevent harm to safety and health when entering and working in confined space.

3. Conduct gas testing to ascertain the atmospheric condition in the confined space is safe for entry in accordance with the applicable legal and other requirements, organisational confined space programme and entry permit system.

4. Participate in risk control and emergency response for entry into and working in a confined space in accordance with legal and other requirements.

5. Monitor the confined space conditions and control measures for safe working.

workplace-safety-and-health-management-i.title: Workplace Safety And Health Management In Marine Industry
workplace-safety-and-health-management-i.efgCourseDuration: 32.0 Hours (29 hours Facilitated Learning (Theory), 3 hours Assessment)

sections.overview:
The Workplace Safety and Health (Shipbuilding and Ship-Repairing) Regulations 2008 (Reg. 6(8)
stipulates that: “It shall be the duty of every person not to oversee or supervise any work
(including any process) in a shipyard or on board a ship in a harbor unless he has received
adequate safety and health training to ensure that the work that he oversees or supervises can
be carried out safely
sections.audience:
The course is a mandatory training course for individuals who are appointed to supervise the
Workplace Safety and Health at shipyards or the marine industry.
The purpose of this Competency Unit is to enable the WSH Coordinator or Supervisor to
acquire the knowledge and be equipped with the application skills to supervise WSH in the
marine industry. This Competency Unit is designed to focus on building the competency of the
WSH Coordinator or Supervisor in identifying the common hazards and risks in the workplace
and taking appropriate control measures in supervising WSH in the marine industry.
The Competency Unit or Course that can exempt anyone from this unit is the Shipyard
Supervisor Safety Course – SSSC
sections.objectives:
PS1 – Explain and interpret salient WSH legislations and other requirements relevant to the
marine industry
PS2 – Describe the WSH duties and responsibilities of a supervisor in marine industry
PS3 – Conduct a briefing on shipyard general safety and explain the Vessel Safety Coordination
Committee (VSCC) and Permit-to-Work (PTW) procedure to shipyard workers and
contractors
PS4 – Identify WSH hazards, evaluate and control risks in marine industry in accordance with
risk management process
PS5 – Carry out WSH inspections in accordance with organisational inspection procedure
PS6 – Conduct incident investigation and prepare report in accordance with legal and other
organisational requirements
sections.prerequisites:
• Employability Skills (ES) Workplace Literacy level 4 and above.
  • Be able to manipulate numbers at a proficiency level equivalent to ES Workplace
  • Numeracy level 4 and above, equivalent
sections.assessment:
Written Assessment (WA) : 100 MCQ & Case Scenario Base Assessement

Learner must achieve Written Assessment 65%
sections.methodology: Slides presentation, sharing of best practice and incident. Risk Assessment and Inspection Report
sections.certifications: Upon successful completion of the course a Statement of Attainment (SOA) will be awarded by SkillsFuture Singapore Agency (SSG)

sections.overview: This course is intended for all workers carrying out tunneling works in building and engineering construction worksites. The course will familiarize participants with the hazards associated when working in a tunnel. They will then be able to identify the hazards, know the preventive measures to be adopted and understand the importance of observing safety signs and safe work procedures.
sections.audience: All workers required to carry out tunneling works in their daily operations and activities exposing themselves to hazards arising from tunnels.
sections.objectives:
This course is aimed at providing individuals with the requisite Tunneling knowledge and skills so as to perform their Tunneling activities properly and safely. At the end of the course, participants will be able to:

  • Understand general safety in Tunneling Works
  • Identify and eliminate hazards working in a Tunnel
  • Practice safety procedures when operating Tunneling Equipment and Machinery
  • Able to work safely in Confined Spaces
  • Practice safety procedure when doing Hot Work and working in Compressed Air
  • Competent in wearing Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
  • Know the dangers and precautionary measures associated with Tunneling Works
sections.prerequisites: As a prerequisite to the course, learners must completed and passed the Apply Workplace Safety and Health in Construction Sites previously known as Construction Safety Orientation Course for Workers.
sections.assessment:
Comprising Theory Session 

Written Test 20 MCQ (WT)

Theory Examination (WT): 60%
sections.methodology: Slides presentation, sharing of best practice and incident. Short Videos.
sections.certifications: Certificates Cards shall be endorsed by EFG Training Services Private Ltd and issued to participants at the end of course.

shipyard-safety-instruction-course-for-w.title: Shipyard Safety Instruction Course For Workers (Hot Work Trade)

sections.overview: The Workplace Safety and Health (Shipbuilding and Ship-repairing) Regulations 2008 Reg. 6 (2) stipulates that “It shall be the duty of every person not to carry out any work deemed hazardous in a shipyard or on board a ship in a harbor unless he has undergone a safety and health training course approved by the commissioner for Workplace Safety and Health”.
sections.audience: All workers required to work at the shipyards in their daily operations and activities exposing themselves to all kinds of hazards.
sections.objectives:
This course is aimed at providing individuals with the requisite shipyard knowledge and skills so as to perform their work activities properly and safely. At the end of the course, participants will be able to:

  • Introduction to Shipyard Safety Instruction Course for Workers (Hot-Work Trade)
  • Know the different types of Hot-Work
  • Know characteristics of flammable substances
  • Understand how vapors / gases can accumulate in confined spaces
  • Understand hoe fires / explosions are caused
  • Understand case studies on two shipyard fire and explosion accidents
  • Know and take safety measures for preventing fire  / explosions
sections.prerequisites:

sections.methodology: Slides presentation, sharing of best practice and incident.

The occupations that this unit would be relevant to include but not limited to:

Workers in the shipbuilding and ship repairing industry. The “Apply Workplace Safety and Health in Shipyard (General Trade) ” is a mandatory WSH training course for workers in general trade operating in the shipbuilding and ship repairing work environment.

PS6. Apply safety precautions when working with different hazards
